title: veronica glauca subsp. kavusica
intro: Veronica glauca subsp. kavusica, also known as the blue foxglove or Kavusica, is a subspecies of the Veronica glauca plant, a perennial flowering plant native to the Balkan Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Veronica glauca subsp. kavusica include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as digestive problems, such as diarrhea and ulcers. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
